title: Epic: OpenCodeHX Haxe port via genes-ts
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-000]

Parent epic for producing a Haxe-authored OpenCode port that emits TypeScript via genes-ts and preserves OpenCode behavior before Caf integration.
acceptance_criteria: Milestone gates M0-M10 are represented as Beads; parity evidence is attached before any replacement recommendation.

title: G0 source inventory and parity matrix
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-001]

Inventory every relevant OpenCode source/test module and classify by area, runtime class, dependencies, tests, extern needs, and genes-ts risk.
acceptance_criteria: A checked-in parity matrix covers packages/opencode/src and priority tests; every module has owner area, runtime class, test oracle, and port priority.
close_reason: accepted: added scripts/inventory/build-parity-matrix.mjs plus docs/opencode-source-inventory.md; checked in reference/opencode-source-parity-matrix.csv covering 523 upstream source files with area/runtime/LOC/externs/genes-ts risks/test oracle/priority/gate fields; checked in reference/opencode-test-priority-matrix.csv covering 187 upstream test files; row-count and CSV quote validation pass; npm run public:precommit passes.

title: Verify genes-ts source/version and choose canonical dependency
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-002]

Confirm standalone genes-ts and Cafetera-vendored genes-ts versions and source diff; choose the dependency path for OpenCodeHX.
acceptance_criteria: Version note states both inspected copies are 1.11.0 with identical common compiler source; standalone repo is pinned as canonical; Cafetera vendor remains integration reference.
close_reason: accepted: verified ../genes and Cafetera vendored genes-ts both report 1.11.0; ../genes/src and Cafetera vendor src are identical by diff -qr with relative-path manifest hash 0691b042d1bda69ca6496ccc534810fa78b24ebcdb28ad309a54421f03bf380e; recorded canonical dependency in reference/genes.pin.json and docs/genes-version-verification.md; npm run public:precommit passes.

title: Initialize OpenCodeHX workspace and Beads/Codex setup
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-003]

Create repo/workspace structure and initialize bd for Codex-driven task execution.
acceptance_criteria: bd init and bd setup codex have been run; AGENTS guidance exists; PRD and seed backlog are committed; bd ready returns initial unblocked work.
close_reason: accepted: bd initialized; seed backlog imported into .beads/issues.jsonl; AGENTS.md guidance added; root project scaffold added; bootstrap docs and Beads state committed in git commit 9470e5c; bd ready returns initial M0 work.

title: Pin upstream OpenCode snapshot and oracle manifest
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-004]

Add the upstream OpenCode snapshot/submodule/worktree used as the behavioral oracle.
acceptance_criteria: A manifest records OpenCode version/snapshot, source paths, test paths, and how to run upstream reference commands.
close_reason: accepted: added reference/upstream-opencode.pin.json and docs/upstream-opencode-oracle.md; records ../opencode commit 69b7f3b8db82c3ab9dacd72d715a57d375de18e4, OpenCode version 1.14.20, source/test/bin/config paths, source/test counts, conditional imports, and upstream reference commands; npm run public:precommit passes.

title: genes-ts NodeNext minimal app smoke
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-005]

Build a minimal Haxe app through genes-ts into split TypeScript and execute it under NodeNext.
acceptance_criteria: Haxe compile passes; src-gen TS exists; tsc passes; node runtime smoke prints scaffold version; generated import specifiers are valid.
close_reason: accepted: added Haxe scaffold, hxml profile, local genes-ts haxe_libraries shim to ../genes, NodeNext tsconfig, npm build/typecheck/smoke scripts, and docs/node-next-smoke.md; npm run build passes; npm run smoke prints 'opencodehx 0.0.0'; generated imports use explicit .js specifiers; npm run public:precommit passes.

title: ts2hx full-project syntax/import audit
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-006]

Run or simulate ts2hx over OpenCode source to identify mechanical conversion wins and hard unsupported constructs.
acceptance_criteria: Audit report lists TS/TSX features, import patterns, unsupported constructs, and recommended manual vs generated conversion areas.
close_reason: accepted: direct ts2hx project probe was run and recorded; it fails before emit on upstream @tsconfig/bun/tsconfig.json, so the full-project audit is simulated with scripts/audit/ts2hx-opencode-audit.mjs; docs/ts2hx-opencode-audit.md summarizes TS/TSX features, import patterns, unsupported/manual constructs, and mechanical vs hand-modeled conversion recommendations; reference/ts2hx-opencode-audit.json contains repeatable counts; npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit, and node scripts/audit/ts2hx-opencode-audit.mjs passed.

title: NPM extern strategy and generator pipeline
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-007]

Define extern/facade strategy for OpenCode runtime dependencies and create a repeatable extern generation or hand-authoring flow.
acceptance_criteria: Extern policy exists for effect, ai, hono, solid/opentui, drizzle, zod, MCP/ACP, jsonrpc, node built-ins; first extern package compiles.
close_reason: accepted: added docs/npm-extern-strategy.md covering effect, ai/@ai-sdk, hono, solid/opentui, drizzle, zod, MCP/ACP, jsonrpc/LSP, PTY, and Node built-ins; added first compiling extern package opencodehx.externs.node.Path plus opencodehx.host.node.NodePath facade; npm run build, npm run smoke, and npm run public:precommit pass; generated TS imports node:path under NodeNext.

title: Host-seam map for Node/Bun/portable slices
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-008]

Map storage, server, PTY, fs, fetch/stream, LSP, terminal, plugin loading, env, clock, and OS seams.
acceptance_criteria: A host seam document exists; each seam has initial Node implementation target and future portability classification.
close_reason: accepted: added docs/host-seam-map.md covering storage, server, PTY, fs, fetch/stream, LSP, terminal/TUI, plugin loading, env, clock, crypto, OS/platform, resources, browser/open, and Node/Bun/portable classifications; initial Node targets and future Bun notes are listed; AGENTS.md points future runtime lessons at the seam map; evidence sampled from upstream global/index.ts, env/index.ts, storage/storage.ts, server adapters, pty/index.ts, and the source parity matrix; npm run public:precommit passed.

title: Effect compatibility strategy
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-009]

Design the minimal Effect extern/facade layer required for config/session/provider compilation without reimplementing Effect.
acceptance_criteria: opencodehx.fx plan exists; first Effect extern/facade compiles; broad Dynamic usage is tracked as debt.
close_reason: accepted: docs/effect-compatibility-strategy.md defines the opencodehx.fx plan; opencodehx.externs.effect.EffectApi and opencodehx.fx.Task compile through genes-ts; npm effect@4.0.0-beta.48 is installed and exercised by the NodeNext smoke; raw Effect Dynamic boundary is tracked as effect-dynamic-001 in docs/genes-ts-limitation-ledger.md; npm run build, npm run smoke, and npm run public:precommit passed.

title: Path alias/resource/import helper support in genes-ts
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-010]

Make sure generated TS can represent OpenCode-style path aliases, JSON/text resources, and dynamic imports.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture covers path aliases, JSON/text import/resource copy, and dynamic import; tsc/runtime smoke passes or compiler beads are created.
close_reason: accepted: genes-ts commit f88d6fb18208b9a5f40031c978162e7fbf8178e7 adds generic TypeScript import-attribute support via genes.ts.Imports.defaultImportWith(...) plus a JSON resource fixture; ../genes gates yarn test:genes-ts and yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots passed; OpenCodeHX now imports #opencodehx/smoke-resource with with { type: json }, copies fixtures/resources into src-gen/resources, and executes it under NodeNext; OpenCodeHX dynamic import fixture executes opencodehx.fixtures.DynamicFixture; npm run build, npm run smoke, and npm run public:precommit passed. Follow-ups filed: opencodehx-6pq for text/file/wasm resource handling and opencodehx-c0j paired with genes-h65 for dynamicImport generated (module: any) typing debt.

title: Port config schema and config loading path
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-011]

Port config discovery, JSONC parsing, schema validation, defaults, and error shape.
acceptance_criteria: Config fixture tests pass against upstream expected outputs for valid config, missing config, env overrides, and validation errors.
close_reason: accepted: Haxe config core added with JSON/JSONC load, strict top-level validation, env/file substitution, jsonc-over-json precedence, legacy TUI key stripping, OPENCODE_CONFIG/OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT overlays, typed share/autoupdate enums, and ConfigSmoke coverage for valid/missing/env/invalid cases. Upstream evidence documented in docs/config-port.md. Compiler blocker fixed generically in ../genes genes-lb1 / commit 7ccc162886aa35e925fdc06fa995058d870f45a6. Gates passed: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit.

title: Port utility/core helper modules
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-012]

Port low-risk utilities needed by config, tools, session, and server.
acceptance_criteria: Selected util modules compile; matching upstream unit tests or fixtures pass; no raw Node APIs outside host seams unless classified.
close_reason: accepted: ported low-risk utilities Format.formatDuration, Lazy<T>, and DataUrl.decode with smoke assertions based on upstream test/util/format.test.ts, lazy.test.ts, and data-url.test.ts; base64 data URL decoding uses opencodehx.host.node.NodeBuffer wrapping node:buffer, so raw Node APIs stay in a host seam; npm run build, npm run smoke, and npm run public:precommit passed; docs/util-port.md records scope and parity notes.

title: Port message v2 and session DTO schemas
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-013]

Port core message/session data structures, part schemas, and serialization helpers.
acceptance_criteria: Message fixtures roundtrip; generated TS types are strict-checkable; upstream session/message test cases are mapped.
close_reason: accepted: Message V2 DTOs decode/encode through Haxe enums; user/assistant/tool/cursor fixtures roundtrip in MessageSmoke; docs map upstream message-v2/schema tests; gates passed: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit

title: Port storage schema and SQLite seam
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-014]

Implement Node-first storage adapter and schema/migration surface for sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Create/read/update session storage fixture passes; adapter shape matches OpenCode conditional seam; no Bun-only dependency required.
close_reason: accepted: Node-first SQLite session store added behind SessionStore; StorageSmoke proves create/read/update session, message/part upsert and hydration, cursor paging, part lookup/removal, and cascade delete; no Bun dependency; gates passed: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit

title: Port file/path/ignore/ripgrep primitives
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-015]

Port filesystem helpers used by tools and config, including ignore handling and search process wrappers.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture workspace tests pass for ignore rules, path normalization, glob/search basics, and error cases.
close_reason: accepted: FileIgnore, Ripgrep, and FileSystem primitives added; FileSmoke covers default/extra/whitelist ignore rules, .gitignore/.ignore flags, path traversal rejection, text read, rg files hidden/glob behavior, and JSON grep parsing; gates passed: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit

title: Port tool schema and registry
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-016]

Port tool input/output schemas, registry, metadata, and invocation surface.
acceptance_criteria: Tool registry compiles; tool schema fixtures validate; unknown/disabled tool errors match upstream shape.
close_reason: accepted: Haxe ToolRegistry added with builtin invalid/glob/grep defs, explicit schema records, typed unknown/disabled/invalid/execution failures, and ToolSmoke coverage for registry ids, schema validation, failure shapes, glob directory rejection/matching, grep directory/file search, and no-match output; gates passed: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit

title: Port read/glob/grep/write/edit/apply_patch tools
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-017]

Port core filesystem tools and patch/edit behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture tests pass for read, glob, grep, write, edit, apply_patch, including failure modes and permission interactions.
close_reason: accepted: read/write/edit/apply_patch core tools added with project-boundary checks, permission request hook, smoke coverage for failure modes and mutating behavior; gates: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit

title: Port bash/shell/PTY safety seam
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-018]

Port shell execution and PTY adapter through a Node-first seam with safe test harness.
acceptance_criteria: Bash tool smoke passes in fixture workspace; timeout, env, cwd, output truncation, and denied permission paths are covered.
close_reason: accepted: bash tool registered with Node process seam; smoke covers output, cwd, inherited env, timeout metadata, truncation, denied bash permission, and denied external workdir permission; gates: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit

title: Port permission model
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-019]

Port permission checks and approval/denial data model used by tools and session flow.
acceptance_criteria: Permission fixture tests cover allow/deny/prompt behavior for file and shell tools; output matches upstream golden shape.
close_reason: accepted: permission rules/config conversion, wildcard evaluation, disabled tool derivation, sync ask/once/always/reject runtime, and read/bash tool integration smoke added; gates: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit

title: Fake LLM provider and transcript parity harness
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-020]

Create a fake provider and golden transcript runner comparing upstream OpenCode with OpenCodeHX.
acceptance_criteria: A credential-free one-turn fixture can run upstream and hx implementations; transcript diff is stable and documented.
close_reason: accepted: fake provider model/info fixture, Haxe transcript harness, upstream-shaped oracle, golden one-turn transcript, and npm transcript:parity comparison added; gates: npm run transcript:parity, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit

title: Headless run command with fake provider
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-021]

Implement enough CLI/session flow for opencodehx run with fake provider.
acceptance_criteria: opencodehx run completes one turn; version/help scaffolds work; fake provider transcript matches golden fixture.
close_reason: accepted: headless CLI scaffold supports --help, --version, run with fake provider default output, and run --format json matching the golden transcript; gates: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run cli:smoke, npm run transcript:parity, npm run public:precommit

title: Session processor one-turn tool lifecycle
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-022]

Port the session processor path for a one-turn request with optional tool call.
acceptance_criteria: Session append, model stream, tool invocation, permission decision, and final assistant output pass fixture tests.
close_reason: accepted: added storage-backed one-turn SessionProcessor with fake provider stream, permission-approved read tool lifecycle, persisted message/part smoke, CLI transcript handoff, and docs. Gates: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run transcript:parity; npm run cli:smoke; npm run public:precommit.

title: Session compaction/retry/overflow handling
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-023]

Port compaction, retry, abort, overflow, and error recovery behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover retryable provider error, abort, context overflow/compaction trigger, and persisted session recovery.

title: Provider registry/models/auth/env
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-024]

Port provider registry, model lookup, auth/env resolution, and config overlays.
acceptance_criteria: Provider/model config fixtures pass; missing auth and env override errors match upstream behavior.
close_reason: accepted: typed provider registry added with ProviderID/ModelID abstracts, typed provider/model/capability/cost/limit/header records, interleaved union modeling, config/env/auth overlays, provider/model filters, custom provider/model alias handling, Bedrock config/env/auth normalization, provider errors, and ProviderSmoke coverage. Docs and test matrix updated; follow-ups filed opencodehx-brn/genes-cn4 for performance-oriented ES6 and opencodehx-8n0/genes-oih for generated TS output polish. Gates: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run format:haxe; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run cli:smoke; npm run transcript:parity; npm run public:precommit; npm audit --json.

title: AI SDK streaming provider integration
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-025]

Wire at least one real provider path through AI SDK externs/facades and generated TS.
acceptance_criteria: A real provider can stream text/tool calls in a smoke test; stream abort/error paths are covered; credentials are optional in CI.

title: Server Hono routes/events/SSE/WebSocket
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-026]

Port server adapter and route/event stream behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Server starts/stops under Node; selected routes return upstream-compatible payloads; SSE/WebSocket event fixtures pass.
close_reason: accepted: Node/Hono server seam added with typed Hono/node-ws/node-server externs, route smoke for health/session/messages/select/abort/SSE, real listener start-stop, WebSocket echo, docs and parity matrix evidence. Gates: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run format:haxe; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run cli:smoke; npm run transcript:parity; npm run public:precommit; npm audit --json.

title: SDK compatibility smoke
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-027]

Run OpenCode SDK or compatible client smoke against OpenCodeHX server.
acceptance_criteria: Client can create/list sessions and receive events; API payloads match fixture expectations.

title: MCP/ACP minimum surfaces
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-028]

Port minimal MCP/ACP server/client surfaces needed by OpenCode tests and main runtime.
acceptance_criteria: MCP/ACP fixture tests pass for discovery, invocation, and error cases selected from upstream.

title: LSP lifecycle/tools
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-029]

Port LSP process lifecycle and tool integration.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture language server can start, answer a basic request, and shut down; errors/timeouts are covered.

title: Plugin loader/install/meta
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-030]

Port plugin discovery, metadata, dynamic loading, and minimum install/invoke flow.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin fixtures pass for local plugin, missing plugin, metadata parse, and invocation path.

title: TUI scaffold render worker and key input
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-031]

Compile and run a minimal OpenTUI/Solid Haxe-authored screen through genes-ts.
acceptance_criteria: TUI scaffold renders a static screen, accepts key input, exits cleanly, and has a generated TS snapshot.
notes: Accepted: Haxe-authored TSX scaffold compiles through genes-ts, strict tsc passes, reference/tui-scaffold.TuiScaffold.tsx snapshots generated output, and npm run tui:scaffold now executes the generated TSX through OpenTUI/Solid using repo-pinned bun@1.3.14 plus scripts/harness/opentui-solid-preload.mjs. The preload mirrors OpenTUI's Solid transform while enabling Babel allowDeclareFields for genes-ts support modules.
close_reason: accepted: npm run tui:scaffold rebuilds Haxe-authored TSX, strict-checks generated TypeScript, verifies the snapshot, runs generated TSX through OpenTUI/Solid with repo-pinned Bun, accepts mock key input, and exits cleanly

title: TUI contexts/routes/theme/keybind
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-032]

Port TUI shared context, route, theme, and keybind foundations.
acceptance_criteria: Static TUI route fixtures render with expected theme/keybind metadata; key dispatch tests pass.
close_reason: accepted: added typed TUI route/theme/keybind foundation; scaffold renders route/theme/keybind metadata through genes-ts default inline markup; leader-key dispatch assertions pass at runtime; gates passed: npm run format:haxe, npm run tui:scaffold, npm run build, npm run test:haxe:unit, npm run smoke, npm test; weak-type scan found no Dynamic/any/unknown/cast/untyped in new TUI sources or generated TUI TSX.

title: TUI session route transcript/tool parts
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-033]

Port session transcript display and tool part rendering.
acceptance_criteria: Terminal snapshot/replay fixture shows user/assistant/tool parts matching upstream shape for fake-provider session.
close_reason: accepted: added typed fake-provider TUI transcript rows for user/tool/assistant/metadata; scaffold renders and asserts transcript shape through OpenTUI/Solid; built-in plugin route navigation now uses a macro-checked constructor against the typed route catalog; AGENTS documents macro-checked string artifacts and using-style helper preference; follow-ups filed opencodehx-vyz and genes-w74. Gates: npm run tui:scaffold; npm run build; npm run test:haxe:unit; npm run smoke; npm test; weak-type scan found no Dynamic/any/unknown/cast/untyped in new TUI source/generated TSX/snapshot.

title: TUI dialogs for model/provider/session/permission
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-034]

Port major TUI dialogs and permission prompts.
acceptance_criteria: Replay tests cover model/provider selection, session selection, and permission approval/denial dialogs.

title: TSX/Solid/OpenTUI genes-ts fixture coverage
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-035]

Add compiler fixtures for TSX/HXX patterns found in OpenCode TUI.
acceptance_criteria: genes-ts test suite has fixtures for props, children, signals/memos, components, imports, and generated TSX accepted by tsc.
close_reason: accepted: ../genes cdb60a3 adds generic TSX fixture coverage for imported signal/memo accessors, imported components with children, spread props, module-scoped JSX namespace import, strict tsc, snapshots, and runtime; gates yarn test:genes-ts:tsx, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, and yarn test:genes-ts passed

title: CLI command surface parity
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-036]

Port remaining CLI commands and help/version/error behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Help text, command aliases/options, and selected command smoke tests match upstream for all in-scope commands.

title: npm/global bin packaging
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-037]

Package generated TS/JS/resources as an npm-installable global bin.
acceptance_criteria: Local npm global install exposes opencodehx; version/help/run/server smoke pass from installed package.

title: Bun binary packaging feasibility
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-038]

Evaluate restoring OpenCode-style Bun single-binary packaging for generated output.
acceptance_criteria: Report covers required asset embedding, workers, platform targets, blockers, and recommended go/no-go for Bun packaging.

title: OpenCode test port matrix
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-039]

Track upstream tests as pass/ported/skipped/deferred with reason.
acceptance_criteria: Matrix includes every packages/opencode/test item; all skipped tests have explicit reason and replacement fixture if needed.
close_reason: accepted: generated reference/opencode-test-port-matrix.csv covering all 187 upstream test items with kind, area, priority, port_status, evidence, defer reason, replacement fixture, and next_bead owner. Added docs/opencode-test-port-matrix.md, npm run inventory:matrix, AGENTS workflow guidance, and formatter follow-up opencodehx-bvh. Gates: npm run inventory:matrix; package JSON parse; CSV validation for complete partial/deferred rows; npm run public:precommit.

title: Performance and UX parity benchmark
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-040]

Measure cold start, fake-provider latency, tool overhead, and TUI input/render latency.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark report compares upstream and OpenCodeHX; regressions above threshold have beads or accepted rationale.

title: Cafex seam inventory mapped to OpenCodeHX capabilities
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-041]

Map Cafex seam ledger concepts to OpenCodeHX extension points without integrating Caf.
acceptance_criteria: Document maps Cafex harness responsibilities to OpenCodeHX capabilities and identifies missing seams for later Caf work.

title: No-Caf-integration guardrail contract
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-042]

Create explicit guardrails preventing Caf-specific logic from entering Phase 1 core OpenCodeHX.
acceptance_criteria: Guardrail doc and review checklist exist; any Caf-related task depends on M9 and cannot block upstream parity work.
close_reason: accepted: added docs/no-caf-integration-guardrail.md with explicit Phase 1 contract, allowed/not-allowed lists, dependency policy, review checklist, and revisit criteria; AGENTS.md links to it; verified Caf-labeled queue has only this guardrail and M9 analysis; npm run public:precommit passes.

title: Portability classification ledger
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-043]

Classify final Haxe source by portable/host/generator-specific and identify haxe.rust/haxe.go blockers.
acceptance_criteria: Ledger exists with file-level classifications and a prioritized list of future portability experiments; no Rust/Go port required.

title: M3 replacement decision checkpoint
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-044]

After headless fake-provider flow, decide whether to continue, pivot, or narrow scope.
acceptance_criteria: Decision note records evidence from M1-M3, genes-ts blockers, generated TS quality, and recommended next milestone.
close_reason: accepted: added docs/m3-replacement-checkpoint.md decision record. Decision is continue OpenCodeHX, defer replacement claims, keep next phase Node-first/server-focused, and track current genes-ts quality risks. Updated docs index, compiler limitation ledger, and genes pin to the compiler commit used by current green gates. Gate: npm run public:precommit.

title: genes-ts limitation ledger and upstream fix loop
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-045]

Maintain a ledger of every genes-ts limitation found during the port and ensure each has repro/fix status.
acceptance_criteria: Ledger or Beads query shows all compiler limitations, related OpenCodeHX blockers, repro status, and fix/accepted-debt status.
close_reason: accepted: added docs/genes-ts-limitation-ledger.md with entry schema, required fields, fix loop, boundary-debt policy, and current empty/open status; AGENTS.md points to the ledger; npm run public:precommit passes.

title: Generated TS readability review gate
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-046]

Define and apply a readability standard for generated TypeScript in high-risk modules.
acceptance_criteria: Review checklist exists; generated TS snapshots for config/session/tool/TUI are reviewed; issues create genes-ts beads.

title: Resource and worker packaging path
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-047]

Port prompt text, JSON resources, parser worker, and TUI worker handling into generated TS build.
acceptance_criteria: Resource manifest and copy/embed flow works for prompts and workers in dev build; packaging blockers documented.

title: Session resume/import/export parity
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-048]

Port session persistence flows beyond one-turn run.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover session resume, list, export/import where upstream supports it; outputs match upstream expected shape.

title: Error taxonomy and user-facing diagnostics parity
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-049]

Ensure generated runtime errors, validation errors, tool errors, and provider errors match OpenCode UX.
acceptance_criteria: Golden tests cover representative errors and compare stderr/stdout/API payload shape with upstream.

title: Periodic upstream rebase procedure
description:
[external_key: opencodehx-050]

Define how to re-sync OpenCodeHX against newer upstream OpenCode snapshots after initial pin.
acceptance_criteria: Procedure documents snapshot update, parity matrix diff, test update, and how to create beads for drift.

title: Prepare repository hooks and private GitHub remote
description: Set up public-readiness hygiene for OpenCodeHX: Haxe formatter config, repo-managed pre-commit hooks, secret scanning, security workflow, and private GitHub repository/remote.
acceptance_criteria: Formatter/security scripts exist and run locally; hooks install through npm script; GitHub private repo fullofcaffeine/opencodehx exists; origin remote is configured; main is pushed.
close_reason: accepted: added hxformat.json, repo-managed pre-commit hook, Haxe formatter guard, staged/full gitleaks wrapper, security workflow, SECURITY.md, package scripts, and hook docs; npm run public:precommit passes; hook installed through npm run hooks:install; private GitHub repo fullofcaffeine/opencodehx created; origin configured and main pushed.

title: Replace bash scanner with tree-sitter and add real PTY lifecycle
description: [external_key: opencodehx-018-followup]\n\nReplace the first string-based bash permission scanner with upstream-style tree-sitter Bash/PowerShell parsing once wasm resource handling is available, and add a real PTY host seam with created/exited/deleted lifecycle events.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover tree-sitter scanner path extraction, PowerShell path forms where applicable, long-lived PTY create/remove events, and generated TS remains strict-checkable.

title: Polish genes-ts output exposed by Hono server seam
description: Discovered from opencodehx-026. The server seam now compiles with narrow Haxe externs, but generated TS still has avoidable output polish issues such as extra temporaries around method calls and unsafeCast wrappers around raw Promise Syntax.code blocks. Reduce to generic genes-ts fixtures before patching ../genes.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal genes-ts fixture captures the server/callback pattern; generated TS preserves useful names, avoids unnecessary tmp variables/casts where type information is available, passes tsc, and OpenCodeHX server output remains strict-checkable.

title: Explore metadata-style async test sugar in genes-ts
description: Discovered from OpenCodeHX Haxe-authored test facade work. genes-ts already supports @:async plus await(expr), but OpenCodeHX tests would be more TS-like and ergonomic with safe metadata-style sugar where practical, such as @:await promiseExpr and/or a macro test form that makes async runner callbacks less noisy. Use ../haxe.elixir.codex AsyncSimple/@:await as reference only; implement generically in ../genes if accepted.
acceptance_criteria: Design or prototype covers what Haxe syntax is actually legal, whether @:await expression metadata can be transformed safely, how runner callback sugar should type Promise-returning bodies without Dynamic/any, and includes minimal genes-ts fixtures plus OpenCodeHX generated-test fixture evidence if implemented.
close_reason: accepted: implemented generically in ../genes as genes-4s2; OpenCodeHX now uses @:await in the generated unit spec and npm run test:haxe:unit passes

title: Add genes-ts Unknown/Undefinable helpers and raw ts.type audit
description: Discovered while porting OpenCodeHX config remote boundaries. Add generic genes-ts helpers for unknown and T | undefined, centralize/audit raw @:ts.type overrides, and then replace OpenCodeHX boundary aliases such as ConfigEnv, MarkdownValue, OpenConfigValue, TomlValue, AssertionValue, ResourceUrl, Buffer, and redundant DynamicAccess record overrides with named helpers, normal Haxe types, externs, or typed decoders. Keep the compiler work project-agnostic; do not add OpenCodeHX-specific knowledge to ../genes.
acceptance_criteria: genes-ts has generic Unknown and Undefinable<T> fixtures/snapshots; OpenCodeHX no longer uses raw @:ts.type for reusable unknown/undefined/index-signature cases; any remaining raw override is named, documented, and present in an audit manifest or Bead follow-up.
close_reason: accepted: genes-ts Unknown/Undefinable helpers landed and pushed in ../genes; OpenCodeHX reusable unknown/undefined/index-signature raw overrides replaced with genes.ts helpers or DynamicAccess; remaining raw @:ts.type seams documented in reference/raw-ts-type-overrides.md. Gates: npm run build; npm run test:haxe:unit; npm test.

title: Add generic genes-ts text/file/WASM resource import helpers
description: opencodehx-6pq added an explicit NodeNext runtime adapter for copied text, file-path, and WASM-named resources. That unblocks OpenCodeHX, but direct Haxe/genes-ts helpers for upstream-style imports would make future ports cleaner: plain text-as-string imports, Bun-style type:file path imports, and dynamic WASM asset imports. This should be reduced and implemented generically in ../genes, using ../genes-vanilla only as read-only reference where regular Genes behavior helps.
acceptance_criteria: A paired genes task or fixture defines helpers/emit behavior for text, file, and WASM resource imports; generated TS remains NodeNext/bundler-friendly; OpenCodeHX can keep Resources as the Node fallback or migrate a smoke fixture to the helper without breaking npm run build/smoke.

title: Support OpenCode text/file/wasm resource imports
description:
[discovered-from: opencodehx-010]

OpenCode uses plain .txt prompt imports, JSON imports with import attributes, WAV imports with type:file, and dynamic WASM imports with import attributes. opencodehx-010 proves JSON import attributes and resource copy under NodeNext, but text/file/wasm resource handling still needs a generic build/runtime plan before session prompts, TUI sounds, and tree-sitter Bash parsing are ported.
acceptance_criteria: Text prompt imports, type:file asset imports, and dynamic WASM imports have fixtures or explicit runtime adapters; NodeNext smoke passes or compiler/runtime limitations are represented as paired genes tasks.
close_reason: accepted: added NodeNext resource adapter opencodehx.resource.Resources for copied text, file-path, and WASM-named assets; ResourceSmoke covers prompt text, WAV file-path existence, and WASM byte access; copy-resources now mirrors fixtures/resources into both src-gen/resources and dist/resources; docs/AGENTS/host seam notes updated. Follow-ups filed: opencodehx-5lx / genes-cbr for generic genes-ts direct resource import helpers. Gates: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run cli:smoke; npm run transcript:parity; npm run public:precommit; npm audit --json.

title: Polish genes-ts output exposed by provider registry
description: Discovered from opencodehx-024. The provider registry Haxe source now models provider IDs, models, capabilities, headers, and interleaved unions precisely, and generated TS strict-checks. The emitted user modules are still less handwritten than desired: repeated temporary declarations, tmpN locals for object literals, unsafeCast noise around Map.get/patch paths, and haxe StringMap.inst access leak into provider code. Reduce these shapes to generic ../genes fixtures and improve output without weakening OpenCodeHX provider types.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal genes-ts fixture captures provider-registry-like typed records, map iteration/get, object literal construction, and patch/merge helpers; generated TS preserves useful names, avoids unnecessary unsafeCast/tmpN noise where type information is available, remains strict-checkable, and OpenCodeHX provider smoke still passes.

title: Tighten config nested schemas
description: Follow-up from opencodehx-011. Replace temporary Dynamic boundaries in ConfigInfo for provider, agent, MCP, formatter, LSP, command, permission, tools, enterprise, compaction, and experimental fields as their owner modules are ported.
acceptance_criteria: Nested config fields used by the next provider/session/server slices have precise Haxe typedefs/enums or documented extern facades; broad Dynamic usage is reduced or ledgered.
close_reason: accepted: provider and permission config are typed at the Haxe boundary, remaining unported nested schemas are documented boundary debt, and npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run public:precommit, npm run test:haxe:unit all pass

title: Track performance-oriented ES6 genes output profile for OpenCodeHX
description: OpenCodeHX should keep idiomatic strict TypeScript as the default generated artifact, but the sibling ../genes compiler should also expose a performance-oriented ES6 JavaScript profile using regular Genes' existing ES6 path as reference. ../genes-vanilla is read-only original-Genes reference only; all compiler changes land in ../genes. Coordinate with the paired genes task and keep this profile secondary until parity evidence justifies using it.
acceptance_criteria: The paired genes issue/design defines how the ES6 profile reuses or adapts regular Genes behavior; OpenCodeHX documents that TS is default and ES6 is performance-oriented secondary; any future OpenCodeHX ES6 smoke is gated separately from the default genes-ts build.

title: Port formatter service parity
description: [discovered_from: opencodehx-039]\n\nThe OpenCode test port matrix separates util/format.test.ts, which is covered by UtilSmoke.formatDuration, from format/format.test.ts, which exercises the full formatter service: formatter config discovery, status, enabled/disabled built-ins, file formatting decisions, parallel formatter checks, and process execution seams. This needs its own port slice rather than being hidden under utility coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover upstream format/format.test.ts status and file-formatting behavior for disabled/no-op, built-in formatter selection, config object handling, disabled related formatters, and process/seam behavior; generated TS strict-checks and npm run smoke passes.

title: Tighten genes dynamicImport generated TS typing
description:
[discovered-from: opencodehx-010]
[paired-genes-task: genes-h65]

OpenCodeHX dynamic import smoke generated `(module: any)` in `src-gen/opencodehx/Main.ts`. The generated code runs, but user modules should not leak `any` when genes-ts can infer the imported module shape or emit a narrow structural type.
acceptance_criteria: genes-ts dynamic import output no longer emits user-module `any` for typed Haxe module imports; a focused fixture or snapshot covers the generated module parameter type; OpenCodeHX dynamic import smoke still passes.
close_reason: accepted: ../genes 899e773 emits unknown dynamicImport callback parameters plus typeof import casts; OpenCodeHX build/smoke and public precommit passed

title: Design Haxe-authored Jest and Playwright test facades
description: Discovered from testing strategy discussion. Define and prototype Haxe-authored test facades for OpenCodeHX that generate idiomatic Jest/Vitest-compatible unit/integration specs and Playwright-compatible browser/TUI/e2e specs, following the successful haxe.ruby pattern: native target tests remain first-class, Haxe adds typed contracts and selector/API/event drift protection, and upstream OpenCode tests stay the behavior oracle.
acceptance_criteria: Design doc covers Jest/Vitest and Playwright lanes, generated target file shapes, Haxe metadata/declaration-host style, typed fixture/selector/API contracts, oracle/differential integration, and initial compiler risks; at least one minimal generated spec fixture is planned or prototyped.
notes: Seeded docs/haxe-authored-testing-strategy.md and AGENTS.md rules from the haxe.ruby testing precedent. Next step is concrete facade/prototype design and generated spec fixture.
close_reason: accepted: documented Haxe-authored test strategy, added Bun-compatible Haxe unit facade prototype, generated async test spec, strict TS check, and npm run test:haxe:unit/build/public:precommit passed

title: OpenTUI Solid runtime gate under supported Bun
description: [discovered-from: opencodehx-031]\n\nThe TSX scaffold compiles and snapshots, but runtime execution is blocked in this environment. Node cannot load @opentui/core because it imports bun:ffi. @opentui/solid@0.1.99 exposes ./jsx-runtime as a .d.ts-only subpath for the Bun preload/build path. The installed Bun 1.0.11 rejects OpenTUI's import attribute type file before the runtime smoke can render. Establish the supported Bun version/build path and make the generated TUI scaffold render, accept key input, and exit cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: Generated TUI scaffold runs under the documented OpenTUI/Solid runtime path, captures a static frame, accepts mock key input, verifies the typed character, and exits cleanly in CI/local gates.
close_reason: accepted: repo-pinned bun@1.3.14 plus scripts/harness/opentui-solid-preload.mjs runs generated TSX through OpenTUI/Solid; npm run tui:scaffold captures static frame, accepts mock key input, verifies typed character, and exits with tui-scaffold:ok

title: Port schema-backed server routes and Bus event stream
description: Discovered from opencodehx-026. Replace the first Hono route smoke with schema-backed request/response DTOs, Bus/AsyncQueue-driven SSE events, upstream error taxonomy, and broader session/global filters from upstream server tests. Keep Haxe route code typed; JSON remains dynamic only at decoders.
acceptance_criteria: Server route fixtures cover schema validation, event queue/heartbeat behavior, session/global filters, and upstream-shaped error responses without broad route-level Dynamic.
close_reason: accepted: typed server protocol DTOs and Hono boundaries; live ServerEventBus-backed SSE with connected/heartbeat/history/live publish coverage; session and experimental global-session filters/cursor fixtures; upstream-shaped error responses; gates npm run format:haxe && npm run build && npm run smoke && npm test

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: added typed ServerProtocol DTO/decoder boundary for Hono JSON, replaced reflective title mutation with typed SessionInfo copy/update, added deterministic per-server session IDs, session list limit/search/start filters, upstream-shaped select/message error responses, and SSE connected/heartbeat event smoke coverage. Gates: npm run build, npm run smoke, npm test. Remaining for full bead acceptance: real Bus/AsyncQueue-backed stream lifecycle and global session routes/filters.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Follow-up in same slice: tightened Hono extern request/response types (req.json -> unknown, Context.json<T> -> Response, app.request -> Response), converted invalid cursor JSON/base64 failures into typed MessageException in MessageCodec, and narrowed server cursor catches to MessageException/StorageException. Gates rerun: npm run format:haxe && npm run build && npm run smoke && npm test.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: added first-pass upstream-shaped GET /experimental/session global session route over the current server session index, with project metadata, limit/search/start/cursor filters, x-next-cursor header behavior, and deterministic updated timestamps for pagination fixtures. Gates: npm run format:haxe && npm run build && npm run smoke; npm test. Remaining: replace snapshot SSE response with real Bus/AsyncQueue-style stream lifecycle and archive mutation coverage.

title: Port tolerant edit replacers and harden apply_patch fixtures
description: [external_key: opencodehx-017-followup]\n\nPort upstream edit fallback replacers (line-trimmed, block-anchor, whitespace/indent/escape/context-aware matching) and expand apply_patch verification fixtures for heredoc input, moves, EOF chunks, malformed patches, and unicode-normalized matching.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover upstream tolerant edit replacement behavior and apply_patch verification failures/move/EOF cases; npm run build and npm run smoke pass.
close_reason: accepted: ported representative upstream edit tolerant fallback matching into EditTool; hardened apply_patch with upstream-shaped type metadata, EOF insertion behavior, Unicode-normalized matching, empty/no-hunk distinction, and smoke fixtures for move, EOF, heredoc, malformed headers, and no-side-effect verification. Updated core-file-tools docs and regenerated test port matrix. Gates: npm run inventory:matrix; npm test; npm run build; npm run smoke.

title: Macro-check known string artifact parameters across OpenCodeHX
description: Discovered from opencodehx-033. TuiRoutes.plugin now proves built-in TUI plugin routes at Haxe compile time, but the same pattern should expand wherever a string argument names a known artifact instead of arbitrary user text: keybind action IDs, tool IDs, provider/model IDs, event discriminants, config keys, resource names, fixture/snapshot targets, and generated file targets. Prefer typed wrappers or small macro-checked constructors so typos fail during Haxe compilation.
acceptance_criteria: Representative string artifact call sites use typed wrappers or macro-checked constructors; remaining naked strings are either arbitrary user/runtime data or filed as explicit follow-up; at least one negative compile fixture or documented macro diagnostic proves typo detection.

title: Project/workspace/install/sync runtime parity
description: Follow-up from opencodehx-zr1. The core config service and discovery acceptance criteria are covered, but upstream project/git/workspace/installation/sync tests still need their owning runtime slices: project/worktree lifecycle, VCS detection, install/npm side effects, sync behavior, and packaging-adjacent command flows. Keep this separate from ConfigLoader so config parsing stays stable while runtime services grow around it.
acceptance_criteria: Upstream project, workspace, git, installation/npm, and sync matrix rows have Haxe-owned fixtures or differential harnesses; config-adjacent runtime behavior is no longer assigned to the closed config Beads; npm run inventory:matrix, build, and smoke pass.

title: genes-ts local map temp collides with result parameter
description:
[discovered_from: opencodehx-022]

SessionProcessor.toTranscript originally used result.messages.map(MessageCodec.encodeWithParts) with a parameter named result. genes-ts emitted a local array named result in the generated TS, colliding with the function parameter and failing strict tsc. OpenCodeHX worked around this with an explicit loop, but genes-ts should fix temp-name hygiene generically.

Evidence: npm run build failed with TS2403/TS7053 in src-gen/opencodehx/session/SessionProcessor.ts before the source reshape.
acceptance_criteria: A reduced genes-ts fixture covers map/temp naming when an enclosing parameter/local is named result; generated TS strict-checks without source contortions.

title: Complete config service and discovery parity
description: Follow-up from opencodehx-011. Port upstream Config service behavior beyond the first parser/loader slice: global config merge/migration, .opencode directory discovery, OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR, plugin origin provenance, remote account/well-known config, dependency install side effects, and update/write path.
acceptance_criteria: Differential or fixture tests cover global/project/.opencode precedence, OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR, schema auto-add without template expansion leaks, plugin origin provenance, and update/write behavior against upstream evidence.
close_reason: accepted: config service/discovery parity criteria are covered by Haxe-owned smoke evidence. ConfigSmoke covers global/project/.opencode precedence, OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR, schema auto-add without token leaks, plugin specs/origins/dedupe/path resolution, update/write behavior, legacy TOML migration, managed config, remote well-known/account config, final normalization, commands/agents/modes, and local plugin discovery. SkillSmoke covers local/remote skill discovery and permission-filtered availability. Remaining runtime surfaces are reassigned: plugin runtime/install/meta to opencodehx-030; project/workspace/git/installation/sync runtime parity to opencodehx-who; real account/provider runtime integration to provider/session beads; raw ts.type cleanup to opencodehx-5ku. Latest gates across the accepted slices passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run format:haxe; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented bounded config discovery parity for ancestor opencode.{json,jsonc}, discovered .opencode/opencode.{json,jsonc}, OPENCODE_DISABLE_PROJECT_CONFIG, and OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR precedence. ConfigSmoke covers these paths. Remaining scope: global config migration, plugin origin provenance, remote/account/well-known config, dependency install side effects, update/write path, and agent/command/skill directory discovery. Gates passed: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented upstream-style best-effort $schema write-back for file-backed configs. The loader parses substituted values but writes schema into the original raw text, preserving {env:...}/{file:...} tokens and swallowing schema write failures like upstream. ConfigSmoke now covers env token preservation and non-leakage. Gates passed: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented typed plugin config specs and origin provenance for string and [specifier, options] entries, with upstream-style later-wins dedupe by package identity or exact file URL. ConfigSmoke now covers plugin merge order, origin alignment, local scope/source provenance, and tuple option preservation. Also fixed generic genes-ts temp-local/catch emission issues in ../genes (pushed commits 42314dc and e43ca07) exposed by this slice. Remaining scope: global config migration/update path, remote/account/well-known config, dependency install side effects, agent/command/skill directory discovery, and plugin runtime loading/path resolution. Gates passed: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented config write/update parity slice. Added ConfigWriter with upstream global file precedence/target selection, local config.json update writes, JSONC-preserving global updates through jsonc-parser, and persisted-config omission of plugin provenance. Generated writer output now keeps the raw writable JSON boundary as unknown/Record<string, unknown> rather than any. Also documented the hard rule that genes-ts fixes must remain project-agnostic. Remaining scope: legacy TOML global config migration, remote/account/well-known config, dependency install side effects, agent/command/skill directory discovery, and plugin runtime loading/path resolution. Gates passed: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented command/agent/mode markdown discovery slice. Added typed ConfigCommand, ConfigAgent, ConfigEntryName, ConfigMarkdown, and ConfigMarkdownFiles modules; ConfigLoader now loads command(s), agent(s), and mode(s) from .opencode and OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR after config files, promotes modes into primary agents, and project-disable skips discovered project entries. ConfigSmoke covers singular/plural command and agent dirs, nested path names, tool-to-permission migration, mode promotion, config-dir command discovery, and project-disable skipping. Generated new modules keep frontmatter as unknown and narrow into typed command/agent records without new any. Remaining scope: legacy TOML global config migration, remote/account/well-known config, dependency install side effects, skill service discovery/loading, and plugin runtime loading/path resolution. Gates passed: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented local skill registry/discovery slice. Added typed SkillRegistry with local .opencode skill(s), external .claude/.agents project and home skills, skills.paths support, dirs, sorted verbose formatting, and missing-frontmatter skip behavior. Config skills is now typed as paths/urls; urls are parsed but remote discovery/download is deferred. SkillSmoke covers local/external/global/path skills, disableExternal, dirs, and sorted verbose output. Remaining scope: legacy TOML global config migration, remote/account/well-known config, dependency install side effects, remote skill URL downloads/cache, permission-filtered available(agent), Effect service integration, and plugin runtime loading/path resolution. Gates passed: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented legacy global TOML config migration parity. ConfigWriter.loadGlobal now loads modern global JSON/JSONC first, then best-effort migrates extensionless legacy config TOML by translating provider+model into model, preserving rest fields through a typed unknown TOML record boundary, writing modern config.json, and removing the legacy file. Added explicit toml dependency, narrow Node fs unlinkSync extern, TOML externs, ConfigSmoke coverage, docs/matrix updates, and AGENTS guidance on typed decoder-style migrations with explicit rest-field preservation. Generated ConfigWriter TS now emits parsed as {[key: string]: unknown}, provider/model as string | null, and no broad any in the migration path. Remaining scope: remote/account config, remote skill URL downloads/cache, runtime plugin loading/path resolution, dependency install side effects, permission-filtered available(agent), and Effect service integration. Gates passed: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented local plugin file discovery parity. ConfigPlugin now scans immediate plugin/*.{ts,js} and plugins/*.{ts,js} roots, normalizes discovered files to file URL specs, and ConfigLoader attaches local provenance for project .opencode directories and global provenance for OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR. ConfigSmoke covers singular/plural plugin dirs, ignored non-js/ts files, file URL specs, and origin scope/source. Tightened PluginOptions generated TS from any to unknown while keeping plugin package options as a documented passthrough. Remaining scope: remote/account config, remote skill URL downloads/cache, runtime plugin module loading and relative path spec resolution, dependency install side effects, permission-filtered available(agent), and Effect service integration. Gates passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented async remote well-known config loading. Added ConfigLoader.loadProjectWithRemoteWellKnown to normalize auth base URLs, inject well-known tokens into the substitution env before parsing, fetch /.well-known/opencode, default missing remote schema, merge remote config before project config, and suppress default-only local username overlay so remote-provided values are not overwritten by defaults. Added typed fetch/well-known externs and ConfigSmoke.runRemote coverage for trailing-slash normalization, token env substitution, and project-over-remote precedence. Main now reports async smoke failures with a catch and runs config-remote-smoke before server-smoke. Remaining scope: remote account/org config, remote skill URL downloads/cache, runtime plugin module loading and relative path spec resolution, dependency install side effects, permission-filtered available(agent), and Effect service integration. Gates passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Follow-up cleanup after GPT 5.5 Pro @:ts.type review: removed the new raw @:ts.type overrides from the remote well-known fetch facade. FetchFunction is now a normal callable typedef over DOM FetchInput/Response, and RemoteConfigObject is plain DynamicAccess<OpenConfigValue>, relying on genes-ts index-signature emission. Left the existing ConfigEnv raw boundary in place because it wants a generic genes.ts.Undefinable<T> helper in ../genes; created opencodehx-5ku to track that project-agnostic compiler cleanup and raw override audit. Gates re-run and passing: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: added active account/org remote config as an explicit async config source. ConfigLoader.loadProjectWithRemoteSources now preserves upstream ordering by merging well-known config, local/project/config-dir/content config, then account config; account token is injected as OPENCODE_CONSOLE_TOKEN before parsing so {env:OPENCODE_CONSOLE_TOKEN} templates resolve in remote account config. ConfigSmoke.runRemote now covers account-config-over-project precedence, token env injection, and provider option substitution. Updated AGENTS/docs/matrix to distinguish this from the still-deferred real account repo/service integration. Gates passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented top-level legacy tools migration into permission config. ConfigInfo now types tools as a string-to-bool map and deep-merges permission maps across config sources; ConfigLoader validates tools entries, normalizes write/edit/patch to edit, converts booleans to allow/deny, and preserves explicit permission overrides. ConfigSmoke covers explicit permission overriding migrated tools plus edit/read migration. Updated config docs and AGENTS with the normalization rule. Gates passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented final config normalization rules. ConfigLoader now applies OPENCODE_PERMISSION after source merge, migrates legacy tools, maps autoshare:true to share:auto only when share is absent, and applies OPENCODE_DISABLE_AUTOCOMPACT / OPENCODE_DISABLE_PRUNE to typed compaction config. ConfigInfo now models compaction's upstream scalar fields instead of leaving that branch fully Dynamic. ConfigSmoke covers env permission override, autoshare migration, compaction flag overrides, and preservation of existing compaction numeric settings. Updated AGENTS/docs with finalization order and scope. Gates passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: added managed config parsing and explicit managed source merge. ConfigManaged.parseManagedPlist strips upstream MDM/mobileconfig metadata keys before normal ConfigLoader validation; LoadOptions can now carry a managedConfig source that merges after user/remote sources and before final normalization. ConfigSmoke covers managed-over-user precedence, server settings, enabled providers, permission rules, and metadata stripping. Platform-specific managed preference discovery remains deferred to a host seam. Gates passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented file-backed plugin path spec resolution parity. ConfigPlugin now normalizes path-like configured specs (file://, relative dot specs, POSIX absolute paths, and Windows-drive absolute paths) relative to the declaring config file, preserves tuple options, resolves package directories, and falls back to index.ts/index.tsx/index.js/index.mjs/index.cjs when no package.json exists. ConfigLoader applies this only for loadFile so virtual/remote config sources do not get fake filesystem bases. ConfigSmoke covers package preservation, relative file specs, package directory specs, index fallback, origin alignment, and direct helper option preservation. Runtime plugin module loading and npm dependency installation remain deferred. Gates passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run format:haxe; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented permission-filtered skill availability. SkillRegistry.available now returns the sorted local skill list and filters through agent permission.skill rules with the skill name as the pattern, reusing PermissionRules so last-match wildcard behavior matches the rest of the port. SkillSmoke covers no-agent sorting, exact skill deny, wildcard deny overridden by a later specific allow, and direct skill deny hiding all skills. Remote skills.urls discovery/cache and final session prompt service integration remain deferred. Gates passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run format:haxe; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Progress: implemented remote skill URL discovery/cache seam. Added SkillRemoteDiscovery.pull to fetch skills index.json, require SKILL.md, download listed files into a contained cache root, normalize trailing-slash URLs, skip missing SKILL.md entries, reuse cached files, and keep path traversal entries out of the cache. SkillRegistry.discoverWithRemote composes local discovery with remote dirs without changing the synchronous local API. SkillSmoke.runRemote uses a typed fake fetch boundary and now runs in the main smoke chain as skill-remote-smoke. Updated docs and the generated upstream test matrix: skill/discovery.test.ts is now partial with SkillRemoteDiscovery evidence; remaining caveats are Effect service integration and binary remote skill resources. Gates passed: npm run inventory:matrix; npm run format:haxe; npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run public:precommit; npm run test:haxe:unit.
